Emory international visiting medical student application
searchProcess.robotsOrAccessNotes: med.emory.edu accessible. Direct hub provided verbatim US + international audience + cost evidence in HTML; PDF policies referenced as supplementary (extraction not invoked this turn since HTML was sufficient).

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Medical Students | Emory School of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Fourth year MD and DO students attending AAMC Member Medical schools in the United States and Puerto Rico can apply

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Medical Students | Emory School of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Students who currently attend an international medical school and are fourth year medical students can apply directly to Emory
candidateFindings.durationDecision: October to February only for international students

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Medical Students | Emory School of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: US/Puerto Rico: $100 application fee + $260 registration fee. International: $525 application fee + $4,500 tuition.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Medical Students | Emory School of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: The TOEFL iBT is required - the minimum score is 20, with no less than 5 scored on each individual section (no exceptions)

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: International students restricted to Oct-Feb window only (verbatim) and must meet TOEFL iBT minimum 20 with no less than 5 per section (verbatim 'no exceptions'). The 5 required core clerkships (IM/peds/surgery/OBGYN/psych) must be passed prior to application.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Emory University Hospital not separately named as placement site on hub (school-level coverage implies the hospital network). Visa support not provided by Emory.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — Emory SOM hub; Emory University Hospital not separately named but the SOM operates clinical placements across Emory Healthcare; (b) two distinct lanes: US LCME/AOA fourth-year ($100 VSLO + $260 reg) AND international fourth-year ($525 + $4,500 tuition); (c) international window restricted to October-February only; (d) TOEFL iBT 20 (5/section, no exceptions) for international; (e) 5 core clerkships passed required for international; (f) visa is student responsibility; (g) 90-day lead time before elective start.
plainEnglishSummary: Emory SOM offers two real lanes. US/Puerto Rico fourth-year MD/DO students apply via VSLO with $100 app fee + $260 registration. International fourth-year medical students apply directly with $525 + $4,500 tuition, must score TOEFL iBT 20 (5/section, no exceptions), must have passed the 5 core clerkships (IM/peds/surgery/OBGYN/psych), and can only rotate October-February. Visa is the student's responsibility.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Georgia gained its first P101 packet covering a confirmed international USCE lane with the strictest TOEFL + core-clerkships requirements captured verbatim, plus the explicit Oct-Feb international window constraint.
